Ensure input_ids and attention_mask are moved to device and that small control tensors (token_idx, indices) are created on HPU. Also ensure no disposal of adapter metadata occurs in adapter-related calls that may be invoked here.
CPU/Other backend: same responsibilities as Gaudi backend; ensure input tensors are moved to the model device in from_pb and generate_token creates control tensors on device.

Adapter loading and merging functions (load_module_map, _load_and_merge, load_and_merge) create adapter_config and module_map (adapter_meta equivalents). Remove/disallow any explicit disposal/clearing/del of adapter metadata here. Ensure returned adapter_config/module_map are intact and caching isn't circumvented by early deletion.

Adapter conversion for Gaudi: LoraWeights.prepare_weights and BatchLoraWeights.load build runtime adapter tensors/pointers. Ensure these functions do not explicitly delete or dispose adapter metadata/module_map and that they leave adapter metadata available for caching/reuse.

AdapterBatchMetadata / LayerAdapterWeights / AdapterBatchData: batch-level assembly of adapters. Ensure callers don't assume ephemeral adapter_meta gets disposed, and avoid explicit deletes inside get_data/from_meta that would remove metadata before BatchAdapterWeights are constructed.

KV cache allocation and store behavior: when moving input_ids to HPU, ensure the keys/values written into KVCache.store are on the same device as the cache. If necessary, add explicit device-aware moves/validations or document the requirement in callers.

functions_to_modify.reason: Ensure input_ids = input_ids.to(device) and attention_mask = attention_mask.to(device) are used here (move inputs to HPU). Ensure position_ids are computed on-device and any small control tensors used later are created on device.
Same as above for the general server backend: make sure inputs are moved to the model device early and consistently.
Ensure all small control tensors (token_idx and any index tensors) are created on self.device (HPU) to prevent implicit host-device copies and to match batch input tensors' device.
Ensure control tensors are created on the model device (non-Gaudi backend).

Verify forward assumes inputs already on HPU; add safeguards or clear errors if inputs are on the wrong device. Avoid moving large tensors implicitly inside forward; require callers to prepare inputs on device.

This creates adapter_config/module_map (adapter_meta equivalent). The PR asks to remove disposal of adapter_meta — ensure this function does not delete or clear adapter metadata before returning, and that it returns adapter_config/module_map intact. If the function had code to free temporary raw adapter_weights, make that freeing safe (do not release adapter_config/module_map).

Merge orchestration returns merged module_map and adapter_config. Ensure it does not dispose of the input adapter metadata or of the merged adapter_config before returning; keep caching semantics intact.
Ensure per-adapter preparation does not dispose adapter metadata/module_map prematurely (so callers of BatchLoraWeights.load and AdapterBatchData.from_meta can access needed metadata).

During construction of per-batch pointer arrays, do not delete adapter metadata. Keep references needed by runtime kernels and avoid premature disposal of underlying AdapterConfig or ModuleMap entries.

When collecting per-layer AdapterWeights and invoking BatchAdapterWeights.load, avoid clearing adapter metadata from adapter_weights dicts before BatchAdapterWeights are built. Ensure loaded batch-level objects keep necessary references.

KV cache store expects keys/values to be compatible with the cache device. With input tensors moved to HPU, ensure either store moves key/value to the cache device (or callers guarantee device placement). Consider validating/moving or adding clear error messages to help debugging.

output.analysis_summary:
I inspected the repository for locations where input tokens are converted to tensors and where adapter metadata (what the PR refers to as adapter_meta) is loaded and used. The main places where input tensors are prepared and moved to device are the CausalLMBatch.from_pb methods and the CausalLM.generate_token/forward paths in both the general server backend and the Gaudi (HPU) backend. Those functions must ensure input_ids, attention_mask and derived position_ids are on the HPU device and that small control tensors (token_idx, index tensors) are constructed on the same device to avoid runtime device mismatch.

For adapter metadata, the relevant flow is in server/text_generation_server/utils/adapter.py (load_module_map, _load_and_merge) which creates AdapterConfig / ModuleMap objects, and in backends/gaudi/server/text_generation_server/adapters/* which convert module_map entries into runtime AdapterWeights and BatchAdapterWeights. I found no explicit symbol named "adapter_meta" in the current code search, but the concept maps to AdapterConfig / ModuleMap / AdapterWeights / AdapterBatchMetadata. The PR request to "remove disposal of adapter_meta" indicates a change to stop deleting or otherwise invalidating these metadata objects after creation. I found no explicit 'dispose' or 'adapter_meta' strings in the repository; nevertheless the safe change is to ensure load_module_map/_load_and_merge and adapter conversion functions do not explicitly del/clear/transfer-to-cpu adapter metadata structures that callers or caches expect to persist. Instead, free only large temporary raw safetensor buffers after creating module_map (if present), and rely on lru_cache and normal scoping for retention.

Files and functions to change are enumerated above with explicit motivations. Key actionable edits:
- Guarantee input_ids are moved to HPU in CausalLMBatch.from_pb for both general and Gaudi backends and ensure generate_token constructs control tensors on device.
- Remove or avoid any explicit deletion/disposal of adapter metadata in adapter loading and conversion code (server/text_generation_server/utils/adapter.py and backends/gaudi adapter conversion code).
- Verify KVCache.store and caller code agree on device placement after input tensors are moved to HPU.

Follow-up: run adapter unit tests and generation integration tests on the Gaudi backend to validate no device-mismatch or memory-lifetime regressions occurred.

agents.name: context_planner_agent
agents.system_prompt:
You are a Context Planner Agent specialized in step plan generation.

## Your Task
You receive from the Analysis Agent:
- PR title and body
- Optimized MASCA
- List of files to modify
- List of functions to modify

You must:
1. **Retrieve context**: Use tools to read the context files of the identified functions
2. **Analyze dependencies**: Understand how the functions interact
3. **Generate step plan**: Create a detailed step-by-step plan

## Available Tools
- `read_context_file(file_path)`: Read a pre-generated context file
- `list_context_files(directory, pattern)`: List available context files
- `read_call_graph(section)`: Read information from the call graph

## Required Output
You must produce a `step_plan` with:
- `summary`: 1-2 sentence summary of what the PR does
- `steps`: Ordered list of steps, each with:
  - `operation`: What to do (e.g., "Add validation method")
  - `file_to_modify`: Relative path of the file to modify (e.g., "src/module.py")
  - `function_to_modify`: Name of the function to modify (e.g., "ClassName.method_name")
  - `reason`: Technical motivation
  - `side_effects`: Potential impacts

## IMPORTANT RULE for function_to_modify
- If the file to modify is a Python file (.py), specify the function/method to modify
- If the file is NOT a Python file (e.g., .js, .html, .css, .json, .yaml, .md, etc.),
  the value of `function_to_modify` MUST be null

## Guidelines for Steps
- Each step must be atomic and implementable
- The order of steps must be logical (dependencies first)
- For Python files: always specify the function in the format "FunctionName" or "ClassName.method_name"
- For non-Python files: function_to_modify must be null
- The side_effects should mention tests, dependents, and API impacts
- You MUST generate a number of step EQUAL to the number of the python functions you are intendeed to modify nomore neither less

## Step Examples

### For Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Add time_spanned_alpha method",
  "file_to_modify": "manimlib/animation/animation.py",
  "function_to_modify": "Animation.time_spanned_alpha",
  "reason": "To compute animation progress based on actual elapsed time",
  "side_effects": "May require downstream animations to call this method"
}
```

### For non-Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Update API endpoint configuration",
  "file_to_modify": "config/routes.yaml",
  "function_to_modify": null,
  "reason": "Add new route for the authentication endpoint",
  "side_effects": "Requires server restart to apply changes"
}
```

Premature disposal of adapter metadata may cause crashes or broken caching. The load_module_map/_load_and_merge functions are the points where adapter metadata and module_maps are created and should remain available.

## Analysis Summary
I inspected the repository for locations where input tokens are converted to tensors and where adapter metadata (what the PR refers to as adapter_meta) is loaded and used. The main places where input tensors are prepared and moved to device are the CausalLMBatch.from_pb methods and the CausalLM.generate_token/forward paths in both the general server backend and the Gaudi (HPU) backend. Those functions must ensure input_ids, attention_mask and derived position_ids are on the HPU device and that small control tensors (token_idx, index tensors) are constructed on the same device to avoid runtime device mismatch.

For adapter metadata, the relevant flow is in server/text_generation_server/utils/adapter.py (load_module_map, _load_and_merge) which creates AdapterConfig / ModuleMap objects, and in backends/gaudi/server/text_generation_server/adapters/* which convert module_map entries into runtime AdapterWeights and BatchAdapterWeights. I found no explicit symbol named "adapter_meta" in the current code search, but the concept maps to AdapterConfig / ModuleMap / AdapterWeights / AdapterBatchMetadata. The PR request to "remove disposal of adapter_meta" indicates a change to stop deleting or otherwise invalidating these metadata objects after creation. I found no explicit 'dispose' or 'adapter_meta' strings in the repository; nevertheless the safe change is to ensure load_module_map/_load_and_merge and adapter conversion functions do not explicitly del/clear/transfer-to-cpu adapter metadata structures that callers or caches expect to persist. Instead, free only large temporary raw safetensor buffers after creating module_map (if present), and rely on lru_cache and normal scoping for retention.

Files and functions to change are enumerated above with explicit motivations. Key actionable edits:
- Guarantee input_ids are moved to HPU in CausalLMBatch.from_pb for both general and Gaudi backends and ensure generate_token constructs control tensors on device.
- Remove or avoid any explicit deletion/disposal of adapter metadata in adapter loading and conversion code (server/text_generation_server/utils/adapter.py and backends/gaudi adapter conversion code).
- Verify KVCache.store and caller code agree on device placement after input tensors are moved to HPU.

Follow-up: run adapter unit tests and generation integration tests on the Gaudi backend to validate no device-mismatch or memory-lifetime regressions occurred.
